title: Chirita
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Chirita-MtW.png]] |name=Chirita |homeworld= |birth=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]{{EwoksDate|the appearance of Chirita|[[Malani the Warrior]]}} {{Starlog|Yearbook Vol. 3|Saturday Morning "Star Wars"}} establishes that Chirita was twenty-two during the events of [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']], means that [[Gender/Legends|she]] must have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. |death= |species=[[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]{{Ewoks|Horville's Hut of Horrors (episode)}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]]{{Ewoks|Malani the Warrior}} |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Grey]] and beige |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=An [[Bright Tree Village tribe|Ewok tribe]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Hi, Chirita. Ready for our canoe ride?''"
"''Oh, I'm sorry, Wicket. I already have a date with Weechee.''"
"''Weechee!|Wicket Warrick and Chirita|}} '''Chirita''' was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who lived in the [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], she participated in the [[Battle of Bright Tree Village (Imperial-Ewok War)|fight]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Raygar]]'s [[Droid/Legends|droids]], although she was no match for them and was defeated. During the [[Ewok Warrior Games]], Chirita defeated the Ewok [[Weechee Warrick]] in wrestling. Afterward, the [[Youngling/Legends|young]] [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]], who had a crush on Chirita, attempted to talk with her, suggesting that the two go on a [[canoe]] ride. However, Chirita did not take his advances seriously. A [[Wokling/Legends|wokling]] Ewok named [[Malani]], who had a crush on Wicket, devised a plan to make him think that she was a warrior girl just like Chirita was. However, it went awry, and Malani ended up hanging onto a [[Tree/Legends|tree]] for her [[Death/Legends|life]], though she was saved by Chirita. Later that [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Wicket walked up to Chirita, inquiring about the canoe ride he had discussed with her. However, Chirita went with Weechee instead. ==Biography== ===Life in the Ewok village=== [[File:Chirita.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Chirita fell on the floor.]] Chirita was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] member of an [[Bright Tree Village tribe|Ewok tribe]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated series, which include the appearance of Chirita in "[[Horville's Hut of Horrors (episode)|Horville's Hut of Horrors]]," to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. she attended the [[Bright Tree Fête]] [[Circus/Legends|carnival]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. [[Gender/Legends|She]] and fellow [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Asha/Legends|Asha]] stood behind the adult Ewok [[Paploo/Legends|Paploo]] as he attempted to smash a circular object by launching a piece of [[Wood/Legends|wood]] into the air with a [[Hammer/Legends|hammer]]. The attempt was successful. During a [[Battle of Bright Tree Village (Imperial-Ewok War)|battle]] in the [[Bright Tree Village]] on Endor, Chirita was one of several Ewoks to try and defend the [[City/Legends|village]] from the [[Imperial Reconnaissance Droid|reconnaissance droids]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Raygar]]. However, she was unsuccessful, and was flung through the air, landing on four bags that were grouped together on the floor.{{Ewoks|Battle for the Sunstar}} ===Ignoring Wicket's pursuits=== {{Quote|Yeah, well, see ya.|Chirita to Wicket Warrick|}} [[File:ChiritaWrestling.png|thumb|left|275px|Chirita wrestles Weechee.]] As an Ewok warrior, Chirita participated in the [[Ewok Warrior Games]]. During the Games, she wrestled the Ewok [[Weechee Warrick]] on a wooden log above a pit of mud before a large crowd. She was able to gain the upper hand, and Warrick fell into the mud, muttering in [[Ewokese/Legends|Ewokese]] in frustration. Chirita cried out in excitement at her victory, and performed a backflip onto a patch of [[Grass/Legends|grass]]. She was immediately greeted by the young Ewok [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]], who was the brother of Weechee. He complimented her on her performance, and Chirita thanked him. He handed her a trophy while telling her that she could add it to her [[Belt of Honor]]. She then promptly walked away from Wicket, who sighed, looking at her as she left. Warrick's friends, [[Latara/Legends|Latara]] and [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], accused him of having a crush on Chirita, which he readily denied. The [[Wokling/Legends|wokling]] [[Malani]] then ran up to Warrick, handing him a basket of [[Sunberry/Legends|sunberries]]. Wicket accepted the basket and ran it over to Chirita, who smiled politely at his offering of the [[Berry|berries]]. ===Rescuing Malani=== {{Quote|Gee, Chirita, that was wonderful. Did I ever tell you about the time I rescued…''"
"''Yes.''"
"''Oh… Uh…|Wicket Warrick and Chirita|}} Malani, who had a crush on Warrick, devised a scheme along with her brother, [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], to make Warrick think she was a warrior just like Chirita. Warrick and Chirita walked by a [[Tree/Legends|tree]] where the scheme was taking place. Just as Warrick inquired about Chirita's interest in [[canoe]] rides, Teebo pretended to be stuck in the tree, while Malani climbed an adjacent tree, intending to "save" him. However, she, too, became stuck in a tree, becoming the one requiring help. She slipped from a branch and nearly fell into the mouth of a [[Snake/Legends|snake]], but she was instead rescued by Chirita, who swung in, hanging on a [[vine]]. She then got the snake to [[Mouth/Legends|bite]] all three of its heads on a [[Staff/Legends|staff]] of hers, and she flung the staff away from the Ewoks. Chirita checked to make sure that Malani was alright. Warrick ran up to praise Chirita's actions. He then attempted to tell her about occasions that he rescued others, only to be reminded that he had already told her. Malani ran off, sobbing that Warrick continued to ignore her. [[File:ChiritaWeechee.png|thumb|right|250px|Chirita had a date with Weechee.]] Later that [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Warrick walked up to Chirita, asking if she was ready for their canoe ride, assuming that she had accepted his offer. She apologized, explaining that she already had a date with Weechee—her boyfriend{{Starlog|Yearbook Vol. 3|Saturday Morning "Star Wars"}}—which Wicket found appalling. Chirita teasingly assured him that he would find another wokling to go with, and bid him goodbye. Indeed, Wicket ended up going for a short ride with Malani. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I'm sure some other Wokling ''[sic]'' will go with you.|Chiritia rejects Wicket Warrick|}} Chirita was quick to defend her village when it was attacked by Doctor Raygar. She did not take seriously the advances of Wicket Warrick, shooting him down each time he attempted to wee her. She was also quick to rescue Malani from being [[Diet/Legends|eaten]] by a snake. Instead of Wicket, she chose to go on a date with his brother, Weechee. She had [[Color/Legends|grey]]-and-beige [[Hair/Legends|fur]]. ==Skills and abilities== Chirita was a skilled warrior, but was no match for Raygar's [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. She was, however, able to successfully defeat Weechee at wrestling as well as swing in on a vine to rescue Malani. She was also capable of performing a backflip. ==Equipment== {{Quote|As wrestling champion, you've earned another trophy for your belt of honor.|Wicket to Chirita|}} Chirita wore an [[Color/Legends|orange]] [[Cape/Legends|cape]] that draped over her head and back. It had a [[Flower/Legends|flower]] on its top. She also wore a Belt of Honor. ==Behind the scenes== Chirita first appeared in "[[Horville's Hut of Horrors (episode)|Horville's Hut of Horrors]]," the sixteenth episode in the [[Ewoks: Season Two|second season]] of the animated television series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']]. The episode was written by [[Paul Dini]] and premiered on [[November 15]], [[1986]].{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} She was identified in the twenty-second and final episode of the season "[[Malani the Warrior]]," which was written by [[Stephen Langford]] and premiered on [[December 13]], 1986. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Chirita}} *{{Ewoks|Horville's Hut of Horrors (episode)}} {{1st}} *{{Ewoks|Battle for the Sunstar}} *{{Ewoks|Malani the Warrior}} {{1stID|Chirita}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Chirita }} [[Category:19 BBY births]] [[Category:Bright Tree tribe]] [[Category:Ewoks]] [[Category:Females]]
